hero_para: You build the transmission most developers won’t touch. On those assets the senior debt is rarely the hold up. It’s the layer beneath it: the first-loss equity that has to be in place before BII or a DFI guarantee will anchor. I structure that layer and bring the equity together, without taking the asset.
subnote: Twenty minutes. On one of your own asset types.
video_caption: A personal note from Sid Mofya at MOTIF 54.
video_slides: The senior debt is rarely the hold up.
It’s the layer underneath. The pre-bankable equity.
I close the gate. So the capital can move.
why_para: Gridworks builds the transmission most developers won’t touch, in markets where that first-loss layer is the hardest piece to bring together. Structuring it and closing it, without taking the asset, is what I do. I would rather show you on one of your own asset types than describe it.
positioning_para: I do not lend, and I do not take the asset. I close the gate: I structure the first-loss equity that committed debt needs to draw and bring it together, and I stay until it is in place. That is the whole job.

sections.title: Where the time actually goes
sections.body: On the transmission and DFI-distribution assets you build, the senior debt and the DFI guarantees tend to arrive late: they need a first-loss layer already in place to anchor to. Bringing that layer together, before the rest of the stack will move, is usually where the time goes.

sections.title: Structure, not capital
sections.body: You have BII on the cap table and structuring muscle in-house, so this is not about capital you cannot reach. The seam that still bites is bringing that first-loss layer together to the point the machinery will commit. I establish whether the gate can close, structure the equity, and bring it together, keeping you inside the structure. I do not lend it, and I do not take the asset.

sections.title: The equity nobody else will hold
sections.body: Across energy, minerals and infrastructure, it is the equity a lender cannot hold and a sponsor cannot easily raise. I establish whether the gate can close, structure the equity that closes it, and bring it together, keeping the local and sovereign owner inside the structure, so committed debt can deploy. I do not lend it, and I do not take the asset.

closing_sub: No deck. I’ll walk you through where the gate sits on an asset like yours, and how I would close it.
cta_label: Twenty minutes with me, no deck
